answer:
**The work is *Andreas* by Cynewulf (work slug: `cynewulf-andreas-07b573`), an Old English poem narrating the apostle Andrew's (Andreas) mission to rescue Matthew from the cannibalistic city of Mermedonia.**[^1]

This occurs in **Chapter 1**, where Andrew approaches the prison: seven guards ("before the barred doors guards were standing, seven together") drop dead at his approach ("Sudden death took them all, they fell, fame-less"). The door opens by divine power, and inside he embraces his fellow apostle Matthew ("Each one embraced the other with his arms, they kissed and they clasped"). Andrew then leads out the captives: **"one hundred and two, counted in number, and also forty, saved from the strife— he left not one of them under the city-lock fastened in fetters—and of women there yet, in addition to the host, one less than fifty he freed, the fearful."**[^2]

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, Chapter 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-74e29a).

[^2]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, Chapter 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-74e29a).
